name: Analysis
description: Real, complex, functional analysis, operator theory, spectral theory

results.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
results.structural_conclusion: Integration and differentiation are inverse operations, revealing that the continuous and the discrete aspects of change are fundamentally unified through accumulation processes.

results.name: Intermediate Value Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Continuous functions on connected domains cannot skip values, establishing that continuity preserves connectedness and revealing the topological nature of real analysis.

results.name: Extreme Value Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Continuous functions on compact sets achieve their extrema, demonstrating that compactness provides the structural constraint needed to guarantee optimization solutions.
results.source_citation: Weierstrass, K. Lectures on function theory (1860s)

results.name: Heine-Borel Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: In Euclidean spaces, compactness is equivalent to being closed and bounded, revealing that geometric finiteness and topological completeness are fundamentally the same structural property.
results.source_citation: Borel, É. Leçons sur la théorie des fonctions (1895)

results.name: Cauchy-Riemann Equations
results.structural_conclusion: Complex differentiability requires satisfaction of a rigid system of partial differential equations, showing that holomorphic functions have an infinitely more constrained structure than real differentiable functions.
results.source_citation: Riemann, B. Grundlagen für eine allgemeine Theorie der Functionen (1851)

results.name: Cauchy's Integral Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Holomorphic functions have path-independent integrals over closed curves, revealing that complex analyticity creates a topological rigidity that eliminates local irregularities.
results.source_citation: Cauchy, A.L. Mémoire sur les intégrales définies (1825)

results.name: Residue Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Global properties of holomorphic functions are completely determined by their local singularities, demonstrating that complex analysis exhibits a profound local-to-global principle.
results.source_citation: Cauchy, A.L. Sur un nouveau genre de calcul (1826)

results.name: Liouville's Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Bounded entire functions must be constant, showing that global boundedness and local analyticity together impose maximal rigidity on complex functions.
results.source_citation: Liouville, J. Leçons sur les fonctions doublement périodiques (1844)

results.name: Riemann Mapping Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: All simply connected proper subsets of the complex plane are conformally equivalent to the unit disk, revealing that complex analysis reduces all planar geometry to a single canonical form.
results.source_citation: Riemann, B. Grundlagen für eine allgemeine Theorie der Functionen (1851)

results.name: Lebesgue Dominated Convergence Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Integration and limits commute under domination conditions, establishing that measure theory provides the correct structural framework for handling infinite processes in analysis.

results.name: Riesz Representation Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Continuous linear functionals on Hilbert spaces are represented by inner products, revealing that geometric structure (inner product) and analytic structure (continuous functionals) are equivalent.
results.source_citation: Riesz, F. Sur les opérations fonctionnelles linéaires (1907)

results.name: Hahn-Banach Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Linear functionals can always be extended while preserving their norm, demonstrating that the dual space structure is as rich as the original space regardless of geometric constraints.
results.source_citation: Banach, S. Sur les opérations dans les ensembles abstraits (1929)

results.name: Uniform Boundedness Principle
results.structural_conclusion: Pointwise boundedness of operators implies uniform boundedness, showing that completeness creates automatic regularity that prevents pathological accumulations of singularities.
results.source_citation: Banach, S. & Steinhaus, H. Sur le principe de la condensation (1927)

results.name: Open Mapping Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Continuous linear surjections between Banach spaces are open maps, revealing that completeness forces topological regularity in the behavior of linear transformations.
results.source_citation: Banach, S. Sur les opérations dans les ensembles abstraits (1929)

results.name: Spectral Theorem for Self-Adjoint Operators
results.structural_conclusion: Self-adjoint operators can be diagonalized through spectral measures, showing that symmetry in infinite dimensions creates the same structural decomposition as in finite dimensions but requires measure-theoretic tools.
results.source_citation: von Neumann, J. Zur Algebra der Funktionaloperationen (1929)

results.name: Stone-Weierstrass Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Algebras of functions satisfying minimal separation conditions are dense in continuous functions, revealing that approximation theory has an algebraic foundation that transcends specific function types.
results.source_citation: Stone, M.H. Applications of Boolean algebras to topology (1937)

structural_conclusion: Analysis reveals that continuity, completeness, and compactness are the fundamental organizing principles that create rigidity and regularity in infinite-dimensional spaces. The field demonstrates that local properties (differentiability, boundedness) interact with global topological structure to produce powerful constraints, while measure theory provides the correct framework for handling limiting processes. Complex analysis shows that additional structure (holomorphicity) creates extraordinary rigidity, reducing geometric diversity to canonical forms.
